decision_target: current ownership versus a new ownership primitive
source_of_truth: Saved activation probe, current Lite and SDK source, and focused current-source tests
read_method: Run the saved replay checker and compare its JSON output with probe-result.json
questions_answered: Per-exec tags are visible to the entry flow, nested controller flows, and execution extensions
An entry flow resolves one current-owned session resource and nested controller flows reuse the same runtime
Sibling entry-flow exec calls receive different current-owned runtimes and do not leak their tags
Explicit checkpointing can save an open durable record before activation cleanup
A later exec can load, bind, and resume the durable record without retaining an ExecutionContext
Current cleanup makes no store commit but does change the activation runtime and its record status to finishing
questions_unanswered: The exact deactivation cleanup contract after removing the finishing transition belongs to progression after acceptance
Parent cancellation and descendant joining remain DKR-2
Safe observation projection and terminal trace behavior remain DKR-3
decision: candidate only: keep current ownership for one top-level exec per activation; do not add nearest-explicit-context ownership; reshape session cleanup so activation close deactivates without beginning durable finish

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Activation probe runtime and store call trace
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Replay close without finish and count runtime record status transitions or store commits

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Overlapping generic activation and SDK sibling-exec probe
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Replay and compare runtime identity and visible tags across sibling activations
Confirm the decision proposes no new ownership primitive and all ownership claims replay
No independent DKR-1 replay exists yet

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Packet-authorized paths, content hashes, replay checker, and governed worker report
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Inspect changed paths and replay the hashed probe artifacts

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Independent checkpoint audit and deterministic replay
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Independent validator checks every claim, wall entry, hash, and replay result

wall_gate.reason: Cleanup changes activation business state, and independent replay is still pending

risk_or_anti_goal_implications: AG-SESSION-ISOLATION risk falls because sibling exec calls have separate runtimes and inherited tags remain activation-local
AG-PRIMITIVE-PROOF risk falls because no new ownership primitive is needed for the tested activation model
AG-CLEANUP-EFFECT is breached by runtime.shutdown calling beginFinish during ordinary activation cleanup even though no store commit occurs
AG-INDEPENDENCE and AG-INTEGRITY keep downstream work blocked until separate replay
